SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description1Txt: BETTER FUTURE FORWARD: BRIDGE LOAN AND LLC FUNDS TO FUND STUDENT/FUNDER "INCOME SHARING" AGREEMENTS
SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description2Txt: SIXUP: SHORT-TERM, RENEWABLE LOAN FOR LOW INTEREST LOANS TO HIGH-ACHIEVING STUDENTS WITH FINANCIAL NEED

ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.FormAndInfoAndMaterialsTxt: PLEASE VISIT OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.JKCF.ORG FOR THE MOST CURRENT INFORMATION ON APPLICATION FORMS AND WHERE TO SUBMIT APPLICATIONS.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.SubmissionDeadlinesTxt: PLEASE VISIT OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.JKCF.ORG FOR THE MOST CURRENT INFORMATION ON SUBMISSION DEADLINES.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.RestrictionsOnAwardsTxt: PLEASE VISIT OUR WEBSITE AT WWW.JKCF.ORG FOR THE MOST CURRENT INFORMATION ON ELIGIBILITY OF AWARDS.

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: Youth Leadership Award for Exemplary Achievement
To support summer fellowships for low-income high school students to conduct laboratory research.
To support the 6th annual DC-Area High School Ethics Bowl.
To support a convening organized by the Collaborative for Access & Successful College Outcomes (CASCO) taking place June 18-20.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Boys and Girls Club of Fauquier

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the Full STEAM Ahead! Program, which provides project based STEM activities for low-income youth ages 5 - 18.
To support annual publication of the Catalogue for Philanthropy.
To support and implement a student engagement program to increase the number of low-income students pursuing their education.
To support the 10th Annual Adviser Summit in December 2017.
To support the rigorous summer academy program and college admissions test preparation.
To support high ability, low-income students through Project Launch at Duke University TIP.
To support feature articles and news coverage highlighting the experience of high-achieving, low-income students in Education Week.
To support the Jack Kent Cooke Young Artist Award which provides $5,000 to $20,000 to 20 exceptional young musicians to advance their studies.
To support STEM career exploration programming and college counseling for high-achieving DC Public School Students.
To support the Postsecondary Access and Attainment (PSA2) Impact Group dedicated to improving postsecondary access and attainment for students.
To support under-resourced students living in the Chicago area.
To support reasearch on the successful transition of first-year students at community colleges.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Leadership Enterprise for a Diverse America (LEDA)

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support college access programming and LEDA's annual Summer Institute.
To support in-school curriculum and an abroad experience for high school students.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Loudoun Education Foundation - John Champe High Sc

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support a college access program for first generation, low-income students through the purchase of Google Chromebooks.
To support STEM academic programming for high-ability, low-income middle school students.
To support the Morven Park Center for Civic Education in providing programming.
To support education reporting, with particular attention towards the experiences of high-achieving, low-income students.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Northern Virginia Community College Educational Fo

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the Pathway to the Baccalaureate Program, supporting high-achieving, low-income students.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Passion for Learning Inc (Good Neighbor Grants)

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support after-school college access programming for under-resourced public middle school students.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Peale Center for Baltimore History and Architectur

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support inquiry-based STEM programming for children in under-resourced communities.
To support the annual American Journey Awards, 9+ Ron Brown Scholars.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Savannah College of Art and Design Atlanta

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the Advocate Grant Program, ensuring underrepresented students receive the support and mentorship.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Southwest Neighborhood Assembly (Book Club for Kid

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support +6 Book Club for Kids podcast.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Teachers College Columbia University (John W Hyl

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the John W. Hyland Jr. Scholarship fund.
To support Telluride Association Sophomore Seminars and summer programming (TASS) and (TASP) for juniors, college level seminars for high-achieving, low-income students.
To support the #RealCollege National Convening, a conference on college student food and housing insecurity.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Art of Problem Solving Foundation (SEP) Bridge

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support a five-week summer math program for underserved, high-potential 6th grade New York City public school students.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Fund for the School District of Philadelphia

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support extracurricular STEM programming for low-income middle school students that will prepare them for entrance into Carver High School.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Hechinger Report (Teachers College Columbia U

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support feature articles, analysis, and highlight solutions for the inequalities that persist in higher education.
To support program expansion to support more underrepresented Baltimore City high school students and the launch of Innovation Pathway, a new innovative research-internship pathway.
To support scholarships for twice-exceptional, low-to-moderate-income students during the academic year.
To support and sponsor educational events held by Loudoun County Public Schools.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: The Trustees of the University of Pennsylvania
The University of Iowa Belin Blank Center (Wallac

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support the 2018 Wallace Research Symposium on Talent Development held in Baltimore, Maryland.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Thurgood Marshall Academy Public Charter School

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support college access programming at Thurgood Marshall Academy, including SAT prep courses, college visits, workshops and more.
To support scholarships for low-income students with artistic aptitude to attend classical theatre summer camp.
To support the First-Generation, Lower-Income Administrator Conference.

AllOtherProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Desc: TEACHERS COLLEGE: DEVELOPMENT OF AN ONLINE COLLEGE COUNSELLING PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT COURSE

DistributionFromCorpusElection.ElectionDesc: ELECTION UNDER REGULATION 53.4942(a)-3(e)(2) QUALIFYING DISTRIBUTIONS TREATED AS DISTRIBUTIONS OUT OF CORPUS The Jack Kent Cooke Foundation, elects pursuant to Regulation 53.4942(a)-3(e)(2) to treat distributions out of corpus in order to satisfy the distribution requirements of IRC 4942(g)(3) for the current tax year ended May 31, 2018. The foundation manager declares below that the foundation is making this election under Regulation 53.4942(a)-3(e)(2) and the current year distriubtion was treated under Regulation 53.4942(a)-3(e)(2) as a distribution out of corpus as follows: Distributions AMOUNT Current year distribution from corpus 350,629

ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.PurposeOfGrantTxt: Provide high-achieving students with financial need access to affordable student loans
ExpenditureResponsibilityGrp.ResultsOfVerificationTxt: Sixup Lending LLC submitted an initial report of program impact as of June 30, 2018 on August 28, 2018. Sixup also submitted financial reports in December 2018 reflecting the liability for this PRI and anonymized reports confirming that all funds were used to make education loans to high-achieving students with financial need.
